change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API

en.value: Activity 1.1 (G): Communication packages, case studies and outreach activities are developed to raise awareness of Codex standards, food safety and one health, and engage policy makers to support application of Codex standards, with a particular focus on the revised Code of Practice to minimize and contain foodborne AMR and the new Codex Guidelines on Integrated monitoring and Surveillance of Foodborne AMR, as examples of a One health approach.Activity 1.2 (G) A series sustainable training programme, through the holding of several events and tutored courses, is developed to increase capacities at country level to understand and use Codex standards and collect and analyse data on the use and impact of Codex standards, with a particular focus on AMR.Activity 1.3 (G): A mechanism to systematically collect and analyse data on the use of specified Codex standards and assess their impact is established, feeding into the M&E framework of the Global Action Plan on AMR.Activity 1.4(G/R/C): A Global Tool to monitor implementation of AMR-relevant Codex standards into regional and national legislation is developed and piloted at the regional and national levels.Activity 2.1 (G): A set of practical tools are developed to support Member countries in their efforts to apply the Codex Guidelines for integrated monitoring and surveillance of foodborne AMR and related standards.   Activity 2.2 (R/C): A situation analysis and assessment of targeted countries’ surveillance capacities related to AMR is carried-out, using existing assessment tools (e.g.,FAO-ATLASS, etc.) and a plan for costed adjustments to the targeted countries' current surveillance system is provided, based on a collaborative, risk-based approach and in line with country needs.  Activity 2.3 (R/C): Capacities for monitoring and surveillance systems of foodborne AMR in targeted countries, together with tools and guidance on establishment of such systems are strengthened (including reagents to perform AST/bacteriology). Activity 2.4 (R/C): Residue monitoring laboratory network is established to strengthen capacity for monitoring and surveillance systems of foodborne AMR in targeted countries.Activity 2.5 (G): The operationalization of national surveillance networks is supported, including the capacity development and support to Members to collect submit data to InFARM.Activity 3.1 (G): A set of practical tools are developed (including through the revision of practical guidelines and protocols being applied in countries), taking into account the most recent scientific developments, to provide Member countries with comprehensive resources to support their efforts to apply the relevant Codex standards such as the Code of Practice to minimize and contain foodborne AMR and related standards.  Activity 3.2 (R/C): Targeted countries’ capacities are assessed using available tools (i.e., situation analysis; the progressive management Pathway tool on AMR - PMP) to identify their specific needs in relation to managing the risks posed by foodborne AMR, including through the review of policy and legal frameworks, the control functions, the interactions with stakeholders and the science and knowledge base. Activity 3.3 (R/C) Targeted countries are supported to adopt best practice in key areas of foodborne AMR based on existing tools and international guidance.Activity 3.4 (R/C) Selected country’s Regulatory frameworks in the food and agriculture sector are assessed and strengthened, in line with the Codex COP to minimize and contain foodborne AMR, and other relevant Codex guidance.

en.full: Implementation of Codex standards to support containment and reduction of foodborne AMR

version_summary.change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API
